topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The doorbell chimed, a jarring, unwelcome sound in the quiet afternoon. I peered through the peephole, my stomach tightening instantly. A delivery person stood on the porch, holding a medium-sized, brown box addressed, quite clearly, to the previous occupants of apartment 3B. My breath hitched. I hadn't even remembered that unit had been vacated yet. Hesitantly, I unlatched the door, my hand trembling slightly as I took the package. It felt heavy, and the paper wrapping seemed oddly stiff. As I stepped inside, the silence of my own apartment amplified the thumping in my chest. I set the box down on the entryway table, my eyes darting around the hallway as if expecting someone to materialize from the shadows. Every creak of the floorboards sounded like footsteps approaching.

[story 2]

It arrived late, just as the evening shadows lengthened across my living room. A courier, looking hurried and slightly apologetic, handed me a parcel. The label was unmistakable: "To Occupant of 3B." A cold knot formed in my chest, pulling my shoulders up towards my ears. I took the package, the cardboard rough beneath my fingertips, and retreated to the kitchen, locking the door behind me with unnecessary force. I didn't dare look at it. I paced the length of the counter, my movements jerky and quick. Every few seconds, I would stop, my gaze snapping toward the door, waiting for a sound, a knock, anything to confirm that this was not a mistake, not a prank, but something deliberate.

[story 3]

The delivery notification popped up on my phone, an unwelcome intrusion. A package. Addressed to the former resident of the unit above mine. I stared at the screen, my jaw clenching so tightly my molars ached. A sudden wave of unease washed over me, making the air feel thick and hard to draw in. I walked to the front window, peering down at the street below, searching for any sign of who might have left it, or perhaps, who might be watching me. My hands were slick with sweat as I finally moved to retrieve the item, my gait uneven, every step measured with a palpable sense of apprehension.
